title: EEG: First Episode Psychosis vs. Control Resting Task 2
description: This dataset comprises resting-state EEG recordings from two independently collected samples of healthy controls and first-episode psychosis (FEP) individuals. EEG data were acquired using a 60-channel low-impedance 10-10 system cap during 5-minute resting periods. The second sample excludes subjects overlapping with the first acquisition to avoid redundancy. Comprehensive clinical assessments including psychiatric rating scales, cognitive batteries, and socioeconomic measures are provided for all participants.
methods_description: Resting EEG data were recorded for 5 minutes using an Elekta Neuromag Vectorview system with a 60-channel low-impedance 10-10 system electrode cap. Clinical assessments included BPRS, SANS, SAPS, GAF/GAS, SFS, MATRICS Consensus Cognitive Battery, WASI, Hollingshead Four-Factor Index, and medication chlorpromazine equivalency at time of scan.

keywords.term: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
